description: Build Springfield agent adapter boundaries, process execution/runtime primitives, and doctor/onboarding checks for Claude Code, Codex CLI, and Gemini CLI.

userStories.title: Implement agent adapter boundary and resolution rules
userStories.description: Write failing tests first, then define the adapter interface in `internal/core/agents/index.go` and `types.go`, plus per-agent packages under `internal/core/agents/claude`, `codex`, and `gemini` for Claude Code, Codex CLI, and Gemini CLI.
acceptanceCriteria: A public adapter boundary exists for supported agents.
The system can resolve the configured project default agent and a per-plan override when present.
Tests cover detection metadata and adapter selection.
Agent-specific behavior remains isolated behind adapters rather than spread through command code.
userStories.notes: Use `exec.LookPath` or equivalent local binary detection. Keep room for future capability flags without rewriting the public contract.

userStories.title: Implement process execution streaming timeout and cancellation
userStories.description: Add `internal/core/exec/index.go`, `types.go`, `process.go`, `stream.go`, and `timeout.go` so Springfield features share one subprocess execution layer with structured events, timeout handling, and cancellation.
acceptanceCriteria: A public execution module exists for subprocess runs and streamed events.
Timeout and cancellation behavior is tested and deterministic.
Streaming output is structured enough for both CLI and TUI consumers.
Tests cover success, failure, timeout, and cancellation paths.
userStories.notes: Create tests under `tests/exec/`. This runtime layer is a shared foundation for Ralph, Conductor, doctor, and TUI monitoring.

userStories.title: Implement doctor checks and onboarding guidance
userStories.description: Use the adapter and execution layers to implement `internal/features/doctor/index.go`, `types.go`, and `detect.go`, then wire the feature through `cmd/doctor.go` for readiness checks and first-run guidance.
acceptanceCriteria: Doctor can detect the supported local agent CLIs and report availability.
Doctor output includes concrete guidance for missing tools or partial setup.
The doctor flow is exposed through the Springfield CLI and can be reused by later TUI work.
Tests cover healthy, missing, and mixed agent environments.
userStories.notes: Tests live under `tests/doctor/`. Prefer helpful local guidance over generic error output.
